name: Phil Urso
abstract: Phil Urso (2 October 1925, Jersey City, New Jersey — 7 April 2008, Denver, Colorado) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist and composer known for his association with trumpeter Chet Baker. Urso learned clarinet as a child and switched to tenor sax while in high school.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: New Jazz at Carnegie

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: Marty left, Sam Graf came on and Phil Urso came on. ... We had great trumpet players, but there wasn't much for them to play. All the jazz went to the tenors. And Red was the bass player and then Sonny Igoe on drums, and it was a good experience and I enjoyed it very much.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997
